roadmap.milestone_count_rationale: 2 milestones: A beginner with 5 hours/week and a significant gap to an Entry-Level Backend Developer requires a highly focused and condensed roadmap to achieve job readiness within the limited time budget of 64 hours.

vision_profile.current_state: A B.Tech graduate with theoretical knowledge, struggling to apply concepts practically and overwhelmed by technology choices.
vision_profile.main_blocker: Lack of practical coding experience, understanding of databases, and proficiency with popular backend frameworks, leading to low confidence.
vision_profile.top_motivation: To become a proficient backend developer and secure a first job within six months of graduation to start a career in tech.

milestones.label: M01
milestones.o: The learner can write clean, modular Python code, apply object-oriented principles, and build a basic RESTful API endpoint capable of handling requests and returning JSON responses.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 2 modules because this milestone focuses on the absolute core of practical Python for backend development and initial API creation, providing a focused and achievable start for a beginner with limited weekly hours.
milestones.identity_statement: You are building the essential practical coding and API development skills that form the bedrock of any backend career, transforming from a student into a capable builder.

project.title: Simple User Management API
project.description: Build a basic RESTful API for managing users (create, read, update, delete) using a Python web framework. The API should handle requests and return JSON responses, demonstrating core backend logic.
project.deliverable: A functional and documented API endpoint accessible locally, with a GitHub repository containing the source code and clear instructions for setup.
seeded_errors: AI-generated code uses an outdated library for request parsing, leading to unexpected errors for certain content types that the learner must identify and replace.

modules.title: Practical Python & OOP for Backend
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills to cover essential practical Python for backend, including object-oriented design and effective data handling, without overwhelming a beginner.

skills.title: OOP & SOLID Principles
skills.why_this_skill: Mastering OOP and SOLID principles is crucial for writing maintainable, scalable, and testable backend code, a key expectation in the current job market.
lessons: Classes and Objects in Python for Backend
Inheritance and Polymorphism in Practice
Applying SOLID Principles to Backend Design

skills.title: Data Structures
skills.why_this_skill: Understanding and applying efficient data structures and algorithms is vital for optimizing backend performance and handling large datasets effectively.
lessons: Python Dictionaries and Sets for Performance
List Comprehensions and Generators for Data Handling
Basic Algorithm Analysis for Backend Performance

scenario.title: Optimizing Data Retrieval for User Profiles

skills.title: Testing & QA
skills.why_this_skill: Proficiency in testing and debugging is non-negotiable for building reliable backend systems and is a core skill for any developer.
lessons: Unit Testing with Pytest for Backend Logic
Effective Debugging Techniques in Python
Introduction to Test-Driven Development (TDD)

science.desc: A critical backend function is returning incorrect data due to a subtle bug in object state management; identify and fix the issue using debugging tools and unit tests.

modules.title: Introduction to API Development
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills to introduce core API concepts, HTTP, and the basics of building a service layer, enabling the learner to create their first functional endpoint.

skills.title: API Design & Service Layer
skills.why_this_skill: Designing well-structured APIs is fundamental for building interoperable and maintainable backend services, a core skill for any backend role.
lessons: RESTful API Principles and Best Practices
Designing API Endpoints and Resources
Understanding the Service Layer in Backend Architecture

scenario.title: Designing a New API Endpoint for User Profiles

skills.title: HTTP & Web Protocols
skills.why_this_skill: A deep understanding of HTTP is essential for any backend developer to effectively interact with web clients and other services.
lessons: HTTP Methods, Status Codes, and Headers
The Request/Response Cycle in Web Applications
Working with JSON Data Format in APIs

skills.title: Web Framework Basics (e.g., Flask/FastAPI)
skills.why_this_skill: Learning a popular web framework is crucial for rapidly building and structuring backend applications, a core requirement for entry-level roles.
lessons: Introduction to Flask/FastAPI for API Development
Routing, Views, and Request Handling
Building Your First API Endpoint

science.desc: A new API endpoint is returning 404 errors despite correct routing; debug the framework configuration and HTTP request handling to resolve the issue.
Explain the core principles of RESTful API design and how you would structure an API for a simple e-commerce application, including common HTTP methods and status codes.

milestones.label: M02
milestones.o: The learner can design and implement database schemas, integrate a backend service with a relational database, perform CRUD operations, and deploy a basic application to a cloud environment, ready for entry-level roles.
milestones.module_count_rationale: 2 modules to cover essential database integration and basic deployment, enabling the learner to build and ship a data-backed application, crucial for securing an entry-level position.
milestones.identity_statement: You are now capable of building and deploying backend services that interact with data, a crucial skill for entry-level roles, transforming into a job-ready backend developer.

project.title: Task Management API with Database
project.description: Extend the User Management API to include task management, storing tasks in a relational database. Implement CRUD operations for tasks and deploy the entire application to a free-tier cloud service.
project.deliverable: A deployed API endpoint with full database integration, accessible via a public URL, and a GitHub repository with schema migrations, application code, and deployment scripts.
seeded_errors: The AI-generated database schema migration script contains a syntax error for a foreign key constraint, preventing successful database setup that the learner must debug and correct.

modules.title: Database Integration
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills to provide a solid foundation in database design, SQL, and ORM usage, crucial for any data-driven backend application.

skills.title: Database Design & Data Modeling
skills.why_this_skill: Effective database design is foundational for building robust and scalable backend systems, directly impacting application performance and maintainability.
lessons: Relational Database Concepts and Components
Entity-Relationship Modeling for Backend Applications
Normalization Forms and Schema Optimization

scenario.title: Designing a Schema for a New Feature

skills.title: SQL Fundamentals & Queries
skills.why_this_skill: SQL is the universal language for interacting with relational databases, a must-have skill for any backend developer working with data.
lessons: Basic SQL Queries (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE)
Advanced SQL: Joins, Aggregations, and Subqueries
Ensuring Data Integrity with Database Transactions

skills.title: ORM
skills.why_this_skill: ORMs simplify database interactions in backend applications, making development faster and less error-prone, a common tool in modern backend stacks.
lessons: Introduction to ORMs (e.g., SQLAlchemy) in Python
Defining Models and Database Migrations
Performing CRUD Operations with an ORM

skills.title: Foundations of System Design
lessons: Advanced System Design — Part 1
Advanced System Design — Part 2
Advanced System Design — Part 3

skills.title: Distributed Systems
lessons: Distributed Systems — Part 1
Distributed Systems — Part 2
Distributed Systems — Part 3

science.desc: A new feature requires fetching complex data with multiple joins, but the current ORM query is inefficient and causing performance bottlenecks; identify and optimize the query for better performance.

modules.title: Basic Deployment & DevOps Foundations
modules.skill_count_rationale: 3 skills to cover essential deployment concepts, containerization, and basic logging, enabling the learner to get their application live and monitor its health.

skills.title: Docker & Containerization
skills.why_this_skill: Docker is an industry standard for packaging and deploying applications, making it a critical skill for modern backend development and CI/CD pipelines.
lessons: Introduction to Docker and Container Concepts
Building Docker Images for Python Applications
Running and Managing Containers Locally

skills.title: Cloud & Deployment
skills.why_this_skill: Deploying applications to the cloud is a fundamental skill, enabling developers to make their services accessible to users globally.
lessons: Choosing a Cloud Provider for Backend Deployment
Deploying a Containerized Application to a PaaS
Managing Environment Variables and Configuration in Cloud

skills.title: Monitoring & Observability
skills.why_this_skill: Effective logging and monitoring are essential for understanding application behavior, diagnosing issues, and ensuring the reliability of deployed services.
lessons: Implementing Structured Logging in Python Applications
Basic Application Monitoring and Metrics
Fundamentals of Error Tracking and Alerting

skills.title: Core Concepts
lessons: Core Concepts — Part 1
Core Concepts — Part 2
Core Concepts — Part 3

skills.title: Scalability & Performance
lessons: Scalability & Performance — Part 1
Scalability & Performance — Part 2
Scalability & Performance — Part 3

science.desc: A newly deployed application is crashing intermittently in production, but existing logs are not providing enough detail; implement enhanced logging and basic monitoring to diagnose the root cause.

Describe the steps you would take to deploy a simple Python backend application to a cloud platform, from local development to a live service, including containerization and basic monitoring considerations.

capability_gap.recommended_milestones_rationale: gap_score=1.00 (>0.90): extreme gap, at most 7 milestones
capability_gap.recommended_modules_per_milestone_ration: breadth=3 domains (≥3): at most 3 modules per milestone
capability_gap.recommended_skill_density_rationale: domain=software market norm=6 skills max per module

Milestones: gap_score=1.00 (>0.90): extreme gap, at most 7 milestones. Modules: gap_score=1.00 (>0.70): wide gap, at most 4 modules. Skills: gap_score=1.00; gap>0.50 → +1; gap>0.75 → +1; max=5.
